name: TITAN INTERNATIONAL INC
business: Titan International Inc. is a global manufacturer of wheels, tires, and undercarriage systems for off-highway equipment. It sells to original equipment manufacturers (OEMs) and aftermarket customers in the agricultural, earthmoving/construction, and consumer markets under brands including Goodyear Farm Tire, Titan, Carlstar, and Voltyre-Prom.

exposures.sentence: Titan s agricultural wheels, tires, and components are manufactured for use on various agricultural equipment, including tractors, combines, skidders, plows, planters, and irrigation equipment, and are sold directly to OEMs and to the aftermarket through independent distributors, equipment dealers, and Titan s distribution centers.

The Company manufactures wheels, tires, and undercarriage systems and components for various types of OTR earthmoving, mining, military, construction, and forestry equipment, including skid steers, aerial lifts, cranes, graders and levelers, scrapers, self-propelled shovel loaders, articulated dump trucks, load transporters, haul trucks, backhoe loaders, crawler tractors, lattice cranes, shovels, and hydraulic excavators.
The Company manufactures wheels, tires, and undercarriage systems and components for various types of OTR earthmoving, mining, military, construction, and forestry equipment...
Titan Specialty is primarily concentrated in the consumer segment, but also manufactures and sells small to midsize agricultural tires.

The Company has implemented the digitalization of components such as rollers and chains. This technology, named Trust (TrackAdvice undercarriage smart technology) consists of embedding sensors inside the components, making them intelligent and able to provide real time data, supporting decisions on maintenance and effective use of the undercarriage.

catalysts.event: Capital expenditures forecasted between $50 million and $55 million for 2026
catalysts.sentence: Capital expenditures for 2026 are forecasted to be between approximately $50 million and $55 million.
